description_long: This house features a stone exterior with a weathered, mossy texture, giving it an aged and historic appearance. The structure includes a prominent square tower with arched windows and decorative white spires at the top, reminiscent of Gothic Revival architecture. The main body of the house has a steeply pitched gable roof covered in dark shingles, and the facade is adorned with tall, narrow arched windows and a circular rose window above the main entrance. The entryway is accessed by a small set of stone steps and is flanked by two large windows, adding to the symmetrical and formal look. The tower section is visually distinct, with its own set of arched windows and a crenellated parapet. The overall design suggests a blend of residential and ecclesiastical influences, making it stand out in an urban environment.
description: A historic stone house with a tall square tower, arched windows, and a steep gable roof, featuring Gothic Revival details and a mossy, weathered exterior.
description_view_0: Side view showing the steep gable roof and the narrow end wall, with the tower rising behind and minimal window detail visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance with steps, a circular rose window, symmetrical arched windows, and the full height of the tower with decorative spires.
description_view_2: Tower-focused view displaying the tall, arched windows and crenellated parapet, with the large lower window and minimal roof visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the length of the building, the gable roof, and a single tall window, with the tower's side windows and spires visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with tall arched windows and a central rose window
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway with stone steps, arched door, and a decorative circular rose window above, flanked by tall arched windows.
